name: SIGMA - Nslookup PowerShell Download Cradle
author: Sai Prashanth Pulisetti @pulisettis, Aishwarya Singam
description: Detects a powershell download cradle using nslookup. This cradle uses nslookup to extract payloads from DNS records.
query: any where (Data:"*powershell*" and Data:"*nslookup*" and Data:"*[1]*") and (Data like~ ("*-q=txt http*", "*-querytype=txt http*", "*-type=txt http*"))

name: SIGMA - Delete Volume Shadow Copies Via WMI With PowerShell
author: frack113
description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities via PowerShell
false_positives: Legitimate Administrator deletes Shadow Copies using operating systems utilities for legitimate reason
query: any where (Data:"*Get-WmiObject*" and Data:"*Win32_ShadowCopy*") and (Data like~ ("*Delete()*", "*Remove-WmiObject*"))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Downgrade Attack - PowerShell
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Lee Holmes (idea), Harish Segar (improvements)
description: Detects PowerShell downgrade attack by comparing the host versions with the actually used engine version 2.0

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Called from an Executable Version Mismatch
author: Sean Metcalf (source), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell called from an executable by the version mismatch method

name: SIGMA - Netcat The Powershell Version
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may use a non-application layer protocol for communication between host and C2 server or among infected hosts within a network

name: SIGMA - Potential RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.EXE Abuse
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell module creation where the module Contents are set to "function Get-VMRemoteFXPhysicalVideoAdapter". This could be a sign of potential abuse of  the "RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.exe" binary which is known to be vulnerable to module load-order hijacking.

name: SIGMA - Remote PowerShell Session (PS Classic)
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects remote PowerShell sessions

name: SIGMA - Renamed Powershell Under Powershell Channel
author: Harish Segar, frack113
description: Detects a renamed Powershell execution, which is a common technique used to circumvent security controls and bypass detection logic that's dependent on process names and process paths.
query: any where Data:"*HostName=ConsoleHost*" and (not ((Data like~ ("*HostApplication=powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:/Windows/System32/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:/Windows/SysWOW64/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:\\\\WINDOWS\\\\system32\\\\WindowsPowerShell\\\\v1.0\\\\powershell.exe*", "*HostApplication=C:\\\\WINDOWS\\\\SysWOW64\\\\WindowsPowerShell\\\\v1.0\\\\powershell.exe*")) or Data regex~ "HostId=[a-zA-Z0-9-]{36}\s+EngineVersion="))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Download
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download command
false_positives: PowerShell scripts that download content from the Internet

name: SIGMA - Use Get-NetTCPConnection
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of network connections to or from the compromised system they are currently accessing or from remote systems by querying for information over the network.

name: SIGMA - Zip A Folder With PowerShell For Staging In Temp - PowerShell
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects PowerShell scripts that make use of the "Compress-Archive" Cmdlet in order to compress folders and files where the output is stored in a potentially suspicious location that is used often by malware for exfiltration.
An adversary might compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.

name: SIGMA - Tamper Windows Defender - PSClassic
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Attempting to disable scheduled scanning and other parts of Windows Defender ATP or set default actions to allow.
false_positives: Legitimate PowerShell scripts that disable Windows Defender for troubleshooting purposes. Must be investigated.
query: any where Data:"*Set-MpPreference*" and ((Data like~ ("*-dbaf $true*", "*-dbaf 1*", "*-dbm $true*", "*-dbm 1*", "*-dips $true*", "*-dips 1*", "*-DisableArchiveScanning $true*", "*-DisableArchiveScanning 1*", "*-DisableBehaviorMonitoring $true*", "*-DisableBehaviorMonitoring 1*", "*-DisableBlockAtFirstSeen $true*", "*-DisableBlockAtFirstSeen 1*", "*-DisableCatchupFullScan $true*", "*-DisableCatchupFullScan 1*", "*-DisableCatchupQuickScan $true*", "*-DisableCatchupQuickScan 1*", "*-DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem $true*", "*-DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem 1*", "*-DisableIOAVProtection $true*", "*-DisableIOAVProtection 1*", "*-DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true*", "*-DisableRealtimeMonitoring 1*", "*-DisableRemovableDriveScanning $true*", "*-DisableRemovableDriveScanning 1*", "*-DisableScanningMappedNetworkDrivesForFullScan $true*", "*-DisableScanningMappedNetworkDrivesForFullScan 1*", "*-DisableScanningNetworkFiles $true*", "*-DisableScanningNetworkFiles 1*", "*-DisableScriptScanning $true*", "*-DisableScriptScanning 1*", "*-MAPSReporting $false*", "*-MAPSReporting 0*", "*-drdsc $true*", "*-drdsc 1*", "*-drtm $true*", "*-drtm 1*", "*-dscrptsc $true*", "*-dscrptsc 1*", "*-dsmndf $true*", "*-dsmndf 1*", "*-dsnf $true*", "*-dsnf 1*", "*-dss $true*", "*-dss 1*")) or (Data like~ ("*HighThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*htdefac Allow*", "*LowThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*ltdefac Allow*", "*ModerateThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*mtdefac Allow*", "*SevereThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*stdefac Allow*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Non PowerShell WSMAN COM Provider
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Detects suspicious use of the WSMAN provider without PowerShell.exe as the host application.
query: any where Data:"*ProviderName=WSMan*" and (not ((Data like~ ("*HostApplication=powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:/Windows/System32/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell*", "*HostApplication=C:/Windows/SysWOW64/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell*")) or Data regex~ "HostId=[a-zA-Z0-9-]{36}\s+EngineVersion=")) and (not Data:"*HostApplication=C:\\Hexnode\\Hexnode Agent\\Current\\HexnodeAgent.exe*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Active Directory Enumeration Using AD Module - PsModule
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detects usage of the "Import-Module" cmdlet to load the "Microsoft.ActiveDirectory.Management.dl" DLL. Which is often used by attackers to perform AD enumeration.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the library for administrative activity

name: SIGMA - Alternate PowerShell Hosts - PowerShell Module
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects alternate PowerShell hosts potentially bypassing detections looking for powershell.exe
false_positives: Programs using PowerShell directly without invocation of a dedicated interpreter
query: any where ContextInfo:"*" and (not ((ContextInfo like~ ("*= powershell*", "*= C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*= C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*= C:/Windows/System32/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell*", "*= C:/Windows/SysWOW64/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell*", "*= \\\?\?\\C:Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*", "*= \\\?\?\\C:Windows\\SysWOW64\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell*")) or ContextInfo:"*= C:\\WINDOWS\\System32\\sdiagnhost.exe -Embedding*" or ContextInfo:"*ConfigSyncRun.exe*" or ContextInfo:"*C:\\Windows\\system32\\dsac.exe*" or ContextInfo:"*C:\\Windows\\system32\\wsmprovhost.exe -Embedding*" or (Payload like~ ("*Update-Help*", "*Failed to update Help for the module*"))))

name: SIGMA - Bad Opsec Powershell Code Artifacts
author: ok @securonix invrep_de, oscd.community
description:
focuses on trivial artifacts observed in variants of prevalent offensive ps1 payloads, including
Cobalt Strike Beacon, PoshC2, Powerview, Letmein, Empire, Powersploit, and other attack payloads
that often undergo minimal changes by attackers due to bad opsec.
false_positives: Moderate-to-low; Despite the shorter length/lower entropy for some of these, because of high specificity, fp appears to be fairly limited in many environments.

name: SIGMA - Clear PowerShell History - PowerShell Module
author: Ilyas Ochkov, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects keywords that could indicate clearing PowerShell history
query: any where ((Payload:"*Set-PSReadlineOption*" and Payload:"*–HistorySaveStyle*" and Payload:"*SaveNothing*") or (Payload:"*Set-PSReadlineOption*" and Payload:"*-HistorySaveStyle*" and Payload:"*SaveNothing*")) or ((Payload like~ ("*del*", "*Remove-Item*", "*rm*")) and Payload:"*(Get-PSReadlineOption).HistorySavePath*")

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Decompress Commands
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: A General detection for specific decompress commands in PowerShell logs. This could be an adversary decompressing files.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Get-ADDBAccount Usage
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious invocation of the Get-ADDBAccount script that reads from a ntds.dit file and may be used to get access to credentials without using any credential dumpers
query: any where Payload:"*Get-ADDBAccount*" and Payload:"*BootKey *" and Payload:"*DatabasePath *"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Get Clipboard
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: A General detection for the Get-Clipboard commands in PowerShell logs. This could be an adversary capturing clipboard contents.

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Evil-WinRm Execution - PowerShell Module
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of Evil-WinRM via PowerShell Module logs by leveraging the hardcoded strings inside the utility.
query: any where (ContextInfo like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\wsmprovhost.exe*", "*:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\wsmprovhost.exe*")) and ((Payload like~ ("*value=\"(get-location).path*", "*value=\"(get-item*).length*", "*Invoke-Binary *", "*Donut-Loader -process_id*-donutfile*", "*Bypass-4MSI*", "*IEX ([System.Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetString([System.Convert]::FromBase64String($a))).replace('???','')*")) or (Payload:"*$servicios = Get-ItemProperty \"registry::HKLM\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\\"*" and Payload:"*Where-Object {$_.imagepath -notmatch \"system\" -and $_.imagepath -ne $null } | Select-Object pschildname,imagepath*") or (Payload:"*$a +=  \\\"$($_.FullName.Replace('\\','/'))/\\\"}else{  $a += \\\"$($_.FullName.Replace('\\', '/'))\\\" }*" and Payload:"*$a=@();$*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation CLIP+ Launcher - PowerShell Module
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Clip.exe to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Obfuscated IEX Invocation - PowerShell Module
author: Daniel Bohannon (@Mandiant/@FireEye), oscd.community
description: Detects all variations of obfuscated powershell IEX invocation code generated by Invoke-Obfuscation framework from the code block cited in the reference section below
query: any where Payload regex~ "\$PSHome\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$PSHome\[" or Payload regex~ "\$ShellId\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$ShellId\[" or Payload regex~ "\$env:Public\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$env:Public\[" or Payload regex~ "\$env:ComSpec\[(\s*\d{1,3}\s*,){2}" or Payload regex~ "\*mdr\*\W\s*\)\.Name" or Payload regex~ "\$VerbosePreference\.ToString\(" or Payload regex~ "\[String\]\s*\$VerbosePreference"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation STDIN+ Launcher - PowerShell Module
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of stdin to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR+ Launcher - PowerShell Module
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Environment Variables to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation COMPRESS OBFUSCATION - PowerShell Module
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
query: any where (Payload:"*new-object*" and Payload:"*text.encoding]::ascii*") and (Payload like~ ("*system.io.compression.deflatestream*", "*system.io.streamreader*")) and Payload:"*readtoend"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation RUNDLL LAUNCHER - PowerShell Module
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via RUNDLL LAUNCHER
query: any where Payload:"*rundll32.exe*" and Payload:"*shell32.dll*" and Payload:"*shellexec_rundll*" and Payload:"*powershell*"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Stdin - PowerShell Module
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via Stdin in Scripts

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Clip - PowerShell Module
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Clip.exe in Scripts

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - PowerShell Module
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts
query: any where Payload:"*set*" and Payload:"*&&*" and Payload:"*mshta*" and Payload:"*vbscript:createobject*" and Payload:"*.run*" and Payload:"*(window.close)*"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Rundll32 - PowerShell Module
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Rundll32 in Scripts
query: any where (Payload:"*&&*" and Payload:"*rundll32*" and Payload:"*shell32.dll*" and Payload:"*shellexec_rundll*") and (Payload like~ ("*value*", "*invoke*", "*comspec*", "*iex*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR++ LAUNCHER OBFUSCATION - PowerShell Module
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via VAR++ LAUNCHER

name: SIGMA - Malicious PowerShell Commandlets - PoshModule
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Commandlet names from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks

name: SIGMA - Potential RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.EXE Abuse - PowerShell Module
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description: Detects PowerShell module creation where the module Contents are set to "function Get-VMRemoteFXPhysicalVideoAdapter". This could be a sign of potential abuse of the "RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.exe" binary which is known to be vulnerable to module load-order hijacking.

name: SIGMA - Remote PowerShell Session (PS Module)
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Tim Shelton
description: Detects remote PowerShell sessions
query: any where (ContextInfo:"* = ServerRemoteHost *" and ContextInfo:"*wsmprovhost.exe*") and (not ContextInfo:"*\\Windows\\system32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\Modules\\Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive\\Microsoft.PowerShell.Archive.psm1*")

name: SIGMA - AD Groups Or Users Enumeration Using PowerShell - PoshModule
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may attempt to find domain-level groups and permission settings.
The knowledge of domain-level permission groups can help adversaries determine which groups exist and which users belong to a particular group.
Adversaries may use this information to determine which users have elevated permissions, such as domain administrators.
query: any where (Payload:"*get-ADPrincipalGroupMembership*" or ContextInfo:"*get-ADPrincipalGroupMembership*") or ((Payload:"*get-aduser*" and Payload:"*-f *" and Payload:"*-pr *" and Payload:"*DoesNotRequirePreAuth*") or (ContextInfo:"*get-aduser*" and ContextInfo:"*-f *" and ContextInfo:"*-pr *" and ContextInfo:"*DoesNotRequirePreAuth*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Download - PoshModule
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download command
false_positives: PowerShell scripts that download content from the Internet

name: SIGMA - Use Get-NetTCPConnection - PowerShell Module
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of network connections to or from the compromised system they are currently accessing or from remote systems by querying for information over the network.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Invocations - Generic - PowerShell Module
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation command parameters
query: any where (ContextInfo like~ ("* -enc *", "* -EncodedCommand *", "* -ec *")) and (ContextInfo like~ ("* -w hidden *", "* -window hidden *", "* -windowstyle hidden *", "* -w 1 *")) and (ContextInfo like~ ("* -noni *", "* -noninteractive *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Invocations - Specific - PowerShell Module
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation command parameters
query: any where ((ContextInfo:"*-nop*" and ContextInfo:"* -w *" and ContextInfo:"*hidden*" and ContextInfo:"* -c *" and ContextInfo:"*[Convert]::FromBase64String*") or (ContextInfo:"* -w *" and ContextInfo:"*hidden*" and ContextInfo:"*-noni*" and ContextInfo:"*-nop*" and ContextInfo:"* -c *" and ContextInfo:"*iex*" and ContextInfo:"*New-Object*") or (ContextInfo:"* -w *" and ContextInfo:"*hidden*" and ContextInfo:"*-ep*" and ContextInfo:"*bypass*" and ContextInfo:"*-Enc*") or ((ContextInfo:"*powershell*" and ContextInfo:"*reg*" and ContextInfo:"*add*") and (ContextInfo like~ ("*\\software\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\run*", "*\\software\\wow6432node\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\run*", "*\\software\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\policies\\explorer\\run*"))) or (ContextInfo:"*bypass*" and ContextInfo:"*-noprofile*" and ContextInfo:"*-windowstyle*" and ContextInfo:"*hidden*" and ContextInfo:"*new-object*" and ContextInfo:"*system.net.webclient*" and ContextInfo:"*.download*") or (ContextInfo:"*iex*" and ContextInfo:"*New-Object*" and ContextInfo:"*Net.WebClient*" and ContextInfo:"*.Download*")) and (not (ContextInfo like~ ("*(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://community.chocolatey.org/install.ps1*", "*Write-ChocolateyWarning*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Get Local Groups Information
author: frack113
description:
Detects the use of PowerShell modules and cmdlets to gather local group information.
Adversaries may use local system permission groups to determine which groups exist and which users belong to a particular group such as the local administrators group.
query: any where ((Payload like~ ("*get-localgroup *", "*get-localgroupmember *")) or (ContextInfo like~ ("*get-localgroup *", "*get-localgroupmember *"))) or (((Payload like~ ("*get-wmiobject *", "*gwmi *", "*get-ciminstance *", "*gcim *")) or (ContextInfo:"*get-wmiobject *" and ContextInfo:"*gwmi *" and ContextInfo:"*get-ciminstance *" and ContextInfo:"*gcim *")) and (Payload:"*win32_group*" or ContextInfo:"*win32_group*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Computer Machine Password by PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
The Reset-ComputerMachinePassword cmdlet changes the computer account password that the computers use to authenticate to the domain controllers in the domain.
You can use it to reset the password of the local computer.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Get Information for SMB Share - PowerShell Module
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems as a means of identifying sources of information to gather as a precursor for Collection and
to identify potential systems of interest for Lateral Movement.
Networks often contain shared network drives and folders that enable users to access file directories on various systems across a network.

name: SIGMA - Zip A Folder With PowerShell For Staging In Temp  - PowerShell Module
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects PowerShell scripts that make use of the "Compress-Archive" Cmdlet in order to compress folders and files where the output is stored in a potentially suspicious location that is used often by malware for exfiltration.
An adversary might compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.
query: any where ContextInfo:"*Compress-Archive -Path*-DestinationPath $env:TEMP*" and ContextInfo:"*Compress-Archive -Path*-DestinationPath*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" and ContextInfo:"*Compress-Archive -Path*-DestinationPath*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*"

name: SIGMA - SyncAppvPublishingServer Bypass Powershell Restriction - PS Module
author: Ensar Şamil, @sblmsrsn, OSCD Community
description: Detects SyncAppvPublishingServer process execution which usually utilized by adversaries to bypass PowerShell execution restrictions.

name: SIGMA - AADInternals PowerShell Cmdlets Execution - PsScript
author: Austin Songer (@austinsonger), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects ADDInternals Cmdlet execution. A tool for administering Azure AD and Office 365. Which can be abused by threat actors to attack Azure AD or Office 365.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the library for administrative activity

name: SIGMA - Access to Browser Login Data
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may acquire credentials from web browsers by reading files specific to the target browser.
Web browsers commonly save credentials such as website usernames and passwords so that they do not need to be entered manually in the future.
Web browsers typically store the credentials in an encrypted format within a credential store.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Copy-Item*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Destination*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*\\Opera Software\\Opera Stable\\Login Data*", "*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles*", "*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\Default*", "*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Default\\Login Data*", "*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Default\\Login Data For Account*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Active Directory Enumeration Using AD Module - PsScript
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects usage of the "Import-Module" cmdlet to load the "Microsoft.ActiveDirectory.Management.dl" DLL. Which is often used by attackers to perform AD enumeration.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the library for administrative activity

name: SIGMA - Powershell Add Name Resolution Policy Table Rule
author: Borna Talebi
description:
Detects powershell scripts that adds a Name Resolution Policy Table (NRPT) rule for the specified namespace.
This will bypass the default DNS server and uses a specified server for answering the query.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Add-DnsClientNrptRule*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Namesp*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-NameSe*"

name: SIGMA - Add Windows Capability Via PowerShell Script
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "Add-WindowsCapability" cmdlet to add Windows capabilities. Notable capabilities could be "OpenSSH" and others.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the capabilities by administrators or users. Add additional filters accordingly.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Add-WindowsCapability *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Name OpenSSH.*"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell ADRecon Execution
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects execution of ADRecon.ps1 for AD reconnaissance which has been reported to be actively used by FIN7

name: SIGMA - AMSI Bypass Pattern Assembly GetType
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects code fragments found in small and obfuscated AMSI bypass PowerShell scripts
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*[Ref].Assembly.GetType*" and ScriptBlockText:"*SetValue($null,$true)*" and ScriptBlockText:"*NonPublic,Static*"

name: SIGMA - Potential AMSI Bypass Script Using NULL Bits
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of special strings/null bits in order to potentially bypass AMSI functionalities

name: SIGMA - Silence.EDA Detection
author: Alina Stepchenkova, Group-IB, oscd.community
description: Detects Silence EmpireDNSAgent as described in the Group-IP report
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*System.Diagnostics.Process*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Stop-Computer*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Restart-Computer*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Exception in execution*" and ScriptBlockText:"*$cmdargs*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Close-Dnscat2Tunnel*") and (ScriptBlockText:"*set type=$LookupType`nserver*" and ScriptBlockText:"*$Command | nslookup 2>&1 | Out-String*" and ScriptBlockText:"*New-RandomDNSField*" and ScriptBlockText:"*[Convert]::ToString($SYNOptions, 16)*" and ScriptBlockText:"*$Session.Dead = $True*" and ScriptBlockText:"*$Session[\"Driver\"] -eq*")

name: SIGMA - Get-ADUser Enumeration Using UserAccountControl Flags
author: frack113
description: Detects AS-REP roasting is an attack that is often-overlooked. It is not very common as you have to explicitly set accounts that do not require pre-authentication.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ADUser*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Filter*" and ScriptBlockText:"*useraccountcontrol*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-band*" and ScriptBlockText:"*4194304*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Data Exfiltration Via Audio File
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential exfiltration attempt via audio file using PowerShell
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*[System.Math]::*" and ScriptBlockText:"*[IO.FileMode]::*" and ScriptBlockText:"*BinaryWriter*") and (ScriptBlockText:"*0x52*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x49*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x46*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x57*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x41*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x56*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x45*" and ScriptBlockText:"*0xAC*")

name: SIGMA - Automated Collection Command PowerShell
author: frack113
description: Once established within a system or network, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*.doc*", "*.docx*", "*.xls*", "*.xlsx*", "*.ppt*", "*.pptx*", "*.rtf*", "*.pdf*", "*.txt*")) and (ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ChildItem*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Recurse *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Include *")

name: SIGMA - Windows Screen Capture with CopyFromScreen
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may attempt to take screen captures of the desktop to gather information over the course of an operation.
Screen capturing functionality may be included as a feature of a remote access tool used in post-compromise operations

name: SIGMA - Clearing Windows Console History
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Identifies when a user attempts to clear console history. An adversary may clear the command history of a compromised account to conceal the actions undertaken during an intrusion.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Clear-History*" or ((ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Remove-Item*", "*rm*")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*ConsoleHost_history.txt*", "*(Get-PSReadlineOption).HistorySavePath*")))

name: SIGMA - Clear PowerShell History - PowerShell
author: Ilyas Ochkov, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects keywords that could indicate clearing PowerShell history
query: any where ((ScriptBlockText:"*Set-PSReadlineOption*" and ScriptBlockText:"*–HistorySaveStyle*" and ScriptBlockText:"*SaveNothing*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-PSReadlineOption*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-HistorySaveStyle*" and ScriptBlockText:"*SaveNothing*")) or ((ScriptBlockText like~ ("*del*", "*Remove-Item*", "*rm*")) and ScriptBlockText:"*(Get-PSReadlineOption).HistorySavePath*")

name: SIGMA - Powershell Create Scheduled Task
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may abuse the Windows Task Scheduler to perform task scheduling for initial or recurring execution of malicious code
query: any where ((ScriptBlockText like~ ("*New-ScheduledTaskAction*", "*New-ScheduledTaskTrigger*", "*New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal*", "*New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet*", "*New-ScheduledTask*", "*Register-ScheduledTask*")) or (ScriptBlockText:"*Invoke-CimMethod*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ClassName*" and ScriptBlockText:"*PS_ScheduledTask*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-NameSpace*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Root\\Microsoft\\Windows\\TaskScheduler*")) and (not (ScriptBlockText:"*Microsoft.PowerShell.Core\\Export-ModuleMember*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Microsoft.Management.Infrastructure.CimInstance*" and ScriptBlockText:"*__cmdletization_methodParameter*"))

name: SIGMA - Computer Discovery And Export Via Get-ADComputer Cmdlet - PowerShell
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the Get-ADComputer cmdlet to collect computer information and output it to a file
false_positives: Legitimate admin scripts may use the same technique, it's better to exclude specific computers or users who execute these commands or scripts often
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ADComputer *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Filter \**") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* | Select *", "*Out-File*", "*Set-Content*", "*Add-Content*"))

name: SIGMA - Powershell Install a DLL in System Directory
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Uses PowerShell to install/copy a file into a system directory such as "System32" or "SysWOW64"

name: SIGMA - Registry-Free Process Scope COR_PROFILER
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may leverage the COR_PROFILER environment variable to hijack the execution flow of programs that load the .NET CLR.
The COR_PROFILER is a .NET Framework feature which allows developers to specify an unmanaged (or external of .NET) profiling DLL to be loaded into each .NET process that loads the Common Language Runtime (CLR).
These profiliers are designed to monitor, troubleshoot, and debug managed code executed by the .NET CLR.
(Citation: Microsoft Profiling Mar 2017)
(Citation: Microsoft COR_PROFILER Feb 2013)

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Create Local User
author: @ROxPinTeddy
description: Detects creation of a local user via PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Create Volume Shadow Copy with Powershell
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may attempt to access or create a copy of the Active Directory domain database in order to steal credential information
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Win32_ShadowCopy*" and ScriptBlockText:"*).Create(*" and ScriptBlockText:"*ClientAccessible*"

name: SIGMA - Powershell Detect Virtualization Environment
author: frack113, Duc.Le-GTSC
description:
Adversaries may employ various system checks to detect and avoid virtualization and analysis environments.
This may include changing behaviors based on the results of checks for the presence of artifacts indicative of a virtual machine environment (VME) or sandbox

name: SIGMA - DirectorySearcher Powershell Exploitation
author: frack113
description: Enumerates Active Directory to determine computers that are joined to the domain
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object *" and ScriptBlockText:"*System.DirectoryServices.DirectorySearcher*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.PropertiesToLoad.Add*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.findall()*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Properties.name*"

name: SIGMA - Manipulation of User Computer or Group Security Principals Across AD
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may create a domain account to maintain access to victim systems.
Domain accounts are those managed by Active Directory Domain Services where access and permissions are configured across systems and services that are part of that domain..

name: SIGMA - Disable Powershell Command History
author: Ali Alwashali
description: Detects scripts or commands that disabled the Powershell command history by removing psreadline module
false_positives: Legitimate script that disables the command history

name: SIGMA - Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature Command PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
Detect built in PowerShell cmdlet Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature, Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool.
Similar to DISM.exe, this cmdlet is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Online*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-FeatureName*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Windows-Defender-Gui*", "*Windows-Defender-Features*", "*Windows-Defender*", "*Windows-Defender-ApplicationGuard*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential In-Memory Execution Using Reflection.Assembly
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of "Reflection.Assembly" load functions to dynamically load assemblies in memory

name: SIGMA - Potential COM Objects Download Cradles Usage - PS Script
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of COM objects that can be abused to download files in PowerShell by CLSID

name: SIGMA - DSInternals Suspicious PowerShell Cmdlets - ScriptBlock
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution and usage of the DSInternals PowerShell module. Which can be used to perform what might be considered as suspicious activity such as dumping DPAPI backup keys or manipulating NTDS.DIT files.
The DSInternals PowerShell Module exposes several internal features of Active Directory and Azure Active Directory. These include FIDO2 and NGC key auditing, offline ntds.dit file manipulation, password auditing, DC recovery from IFM backups and password hash calculation.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of DSInternals for administration or audit purpose.

name: SIGMA - Dump Credentials from Windows Credential Manager With PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials.
Passwords are stored in several places on a system, depending on the operating system or application holding the credentials.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Get-PasswordVaultCredentials*", "*Get-CredManCreds*")) or (ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Windows.Security.Credentials.PasswordVault*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Microsoft.CSharp.CSharpCodeProvider*" and ScriptBlockText:"*[System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeEnvironment]::GetRuntimeDirectory())*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Collections.ArrayList*" and ScriptBlockText:"*System.CodeDom.Compiler.CompilerParameters*")

name: SIGMA - Enable Windows Remote Management
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to interact with remote systems using Windows Remote Management (WinRM). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious Windows Feature Enabled
author: frack113
description:
Detects usage of the built-in PowerShell cmdlet "Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature" used as a Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool.
Similar to DISM.exe, this cmdlet is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the features listed in the rule.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Online*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-FeatureName*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*TelnetServer*", "*Internet-Explorer-Optional-amd64*", "*TFTP*", "*SMB1Protocol*", "*Client-ProjFS*", "*Microsoft-Windows-Subsystem-Linux*"))

name: SIGMA - Enumerate Credentials from Windows Credential Manager With PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials.
Passwords are stored in several places on a system, depending on the operating system or application holding the credentials.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*vaultcmd*" and ScriptBlockText:"*/listcreds:*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Windows Credentials*", "*Web Credentials*"))

name: SIGMA - Disable of ETW Trace - Powershell
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of powershell cmdlets to disable or remove ETW trace sessions
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Remove-EtwTraceProvider *" or (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-EtwTraceProvider *" and ScriptBlockText:"*0x11*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Mailbox SMTP Forward Rule
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the powerShell Set-Mailbox Cmdlet to set-up an SMTP forwarding rule.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the cmdlet to forward emails
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Set-Mailbox *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -DeliverToMailboxAndForward *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -ForwardingSmtpAddress *"

name: SIGMA - Certificate Exported Via PowerShell - ScriptBlock
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects calls to cmdlets inside of PowerShell scripts that are used to export certificates from the local certificate store. Threat actors were seen abusing this to steal private keys from compromised machines.
false_positives: Legitimate certificate exports by administrators. Additional filters might be required.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious FromBase64String Usage On Gzip Archive - Ps Script
author: frack113
description: Detects attempts of decoding a base64 Gzip archive in a PowerShell script. This technique is often used as a method to load malicious content into memory afterward.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*FromBase64String*" and ScriptBlockText:"*MemoryStream*" and ScriptBlockText:"*H4sI*"

name: SIGMA - Service Registry Permissions Weakness Check
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.
Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start.
Windows stores local service configuration information in the Registry under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Computers Enumeration With Get-AdComputer
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of the "Get-AdComputer" to enumerate Computers or properties within Active Directory.

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Group Enumeration With Get-AdGroup
author: frack113
description: Detects usage of the "Get-AdGroup" cmdlet to enumerate Groups within Active Directory

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Get-ADReplAccount
author: frack113
description:
The DSInternals PowerShell Module exposes several internal features of Active Directory and Azure Active Directory.
These include FIDO2 and NGC key auditing, offline ntds.dit file manipulation, password auditing, DC recovery from IFM backups and password hash calculation.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ADReplAccount*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-All *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Server *"

name: SIGMA - Automated Collection Bookmarks Using Get-ChildItem PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may enumerate browser bookmarks to learn more about compromised hosts.
Browser bookmarks may reveal personal information about users (ex: banking sites, interests, social media, etc.) as well as details about
internal network resources such as servers, tools/dashboards, or other related infrastructure.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ChildItem*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Recurse *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Path *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Filter Bookmarks*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Force*"

name: SIGMA - Security Software Discovery Via Powershell Script
author: frack113, Anish Bogati, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects calls to "get-process" where the output is piped to a "where-object" filter to search for security solution processes.
Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of security software, configurations, defensive tools, and sensors that are installed on a system or in a cloud environment. This may include things such as firewall rules and anti-virus
false_positives: False positives might occur due to the nature of the ScriptBlock being ingested as a big blob. Initial tuning is required.
As the "selection_cmdlet" is common in scripts the matching engine might slow down the search. Change into regex or a more accurate string to avoid heavy resource consumption if experienced
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*get-process | \?*", "*get-process | where*", "*gps | \?*", "*gps | where*")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Company -like*", "*Description -like*", "*Name -like*", "*Path -like*", "*Product -like*")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*\*avira\**", "*\*carbonblack\**", "*\*cylance\**", "*\*defender\**", "*\*kaspersky\**", "*\*malware\**", "*\*sentinel\**", "*\*symantec\**", "*\*virus\**"))

name: SIGMA - HackTool - Rubeus Execution - ScriptBlock
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of the hacktool Rubeus using specific command line flags

name: SIGMA - HackTool - WinPwn Execution - ScriptBlock
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
description: Detects scriptblock text keywords indicative of potential usge of the tool WinPwn. A tool for Windows and Active Directory reconnaissance and exploitation.
false_positives: As the script block is a blob of text. False positive may occur with scripts that contain the keyword as a reference or simply use it for detection.

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Hotfix Enumeration
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects call to "Win32_QuickFixEngineering" in order to enumerate installed hotfixes often used in "enum" scripts by attackers

name: SIGMA - PowerShell ICMP Exfiltration
author: Bartlomiej Czyz @bczyz1, oscd.community
description: Detects Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - ICMP. Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an un-encrypted network protocol other than that of the existing command and control channel.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*System.Net.NetworkInformation.Ping*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.Send(*"

name: SIGMA - Import PowerShell Modules From Suspicious Directories
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects powershell scripts that import modules from suspicious directories
query: any where ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Import-Module \"$Env:Temp\\*", "*Import-Module '$Env:Temp\\*", "*Import-Module $Env:Temp\\*", "*Import-Module \"$Env:Appdata\\*", "*Import-Module '$Env:Appdata\\*", "*Import-Module $Env:Appdata\\*", "*Import-Module C:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*ipmo \"$Env:Temp\\*", "*ipmo '$Env:Temp\\*", "*ipmo $Env:Temp\\*", "*ipmo \"$Env:Appdata\\*", "*ipmo '$Env:Appdata\\*", "*ipmo $Env:Appdata\\*", "*ipmo C:\\Users\\Public\\*")

name: SIGMA - Unsigned AppX Installation Attempt Using Add-AppxPackage - PsScript
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "Add-AppxPackage" or it's alias "Add-AppPackage" to install unsigned AppX packages
false_positives: Installation of unsigned packages for testing purposes

name: SIGMA - Execute Invoke-command on Remote Host
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to interact with remote systems using Windows Remote Management (WinRM). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.

name: SIGMA - Powershell DNSExfiltration
author: frack113
description: DNSExfiltrator allows for transferring (exfiltrate) a file over a DNS request covert channel
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Invoke-DNSExfiltrator*" or (ScriptBlockText:"* -i *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -d *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -p *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -doh *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -t *")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation CLIP+ Launcher - PowerShell
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Clip.exe to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Obfuscated IEX Invocation - PowerShell
author: Daniel Bohannon (@Mandiant/@FireEye), oscd.community
description: Detects all variations of obfuscated powershell IEX invocation code generated by Invoke-Obfuscation framework from the following code block \u2014
query: any where ScriptBlockText regex~ "\$PSHome\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$PSHome\[" or ScriptBlockText regex~ "\$ShellId\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$ShellId\[" or ScriptBlockText regex~ "\$env:Public\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$env:Public\[" or ScriptBlockText regex~ "\$env:ComSpec\[(\s*\d{1,3}\s*,){2}" or ScriptBlockText regex~ "\*mdr\*\W\s*\)\.Name" or ScriptBlockText regex~ "\$VerbosePreference\.ToString\("

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation STDIN+ Launcher - Powershell
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of stdin to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR+ Launcher - PowerShell
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Environment Variables to execute PowerShell

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation COMPRESS OBFUSCATION - PowerShell
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*new-object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*text.encoding]::ascii*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*system.io.compression.deflatestream*", "*system.io.streamreader*")) and ScriptBlockText:"*readtoend"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation RUNDLL LAUNCHER - PowerShell
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via RUNDLL LAUNCHER
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*rundll32.exe*" and ScriptBlockText:"*shell32.dll*" and ScriptBlockText:"*shellexec_rundll*" and ScriptBlockText:"*powershell*"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Stdin - Powershell
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via Stdin in Scripts

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Clip - Powershell
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Clip.exe in Scripts

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - PowerShell
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*set*" and ScriptBlockText:"*&&*" and ScriptBlockText:"*mshta*" and ScriptBlockText:"*vbscript:createobject*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.run*" and ScriptBlockText:"*(window.close)*"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Rundll32 - PowerShell
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Rundll32 in Scripts
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*&&*" and ScriptBlockText:"*rundll32*" and ScriptBlockText:"*shell32.dll*" and ScriptBlockText:"*shellexec_rundll*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*value*", "*invoke*", "*comspec*", "*iex*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR++ LAUNCHER OBFUSCATION - PowerShell
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via VAR++ LAUNCHER

name: SIGMA - Powershell Keylogging
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may log user keystrokes to intercept credentials as the user types them.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Get-Keystrokes*" or (ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ProcAddress user32.dll GetAsyncKeyState*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ProcAddress user32.dll GetForegroundWindow*")

name: SIGMA - Powershell LocalAccount Manipulation
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain access to victim systems.
Account manipulation may consist of any action that preserves adversary access to a compromised account, such as modifying credentials or permission groups

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Mailbox Export to Share - PS
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the powerShell New-MailboxExportRequest Cmdlet to exports a mailbox to a remote or local share, as used in ProxyShell exploitations
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*New-MailboxExportRequest*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Mailbox *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -FilePath \\\\*"

name: SIGMA - Malicious PowerShell Commandlets - ScriptBlock
author: Sean Metcalf, Florian Roth, Bartlomiej Czyz @bczyz1, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali, Tim Shelton, Mustafa Kaan Demir, Georg Lauenstein, Max Altgelt, Tobias Michalski, Austin Songer
description: Detects Commandlet names from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks

name: SIGMA - Malicious PowerShell Keywords
author: Sean Metcalf (source), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects keywords from well-known PowerShell exploitation frameworks
false_positives: Depending on the scripts, this rule might require some initial tuning to fit the environment

name: SIGMA - Live Memory Dump Using Powershell
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of a PowerShell command to dump the live memory of a Windows machine

name: SIGMA - Modify Group Policy Settings - ScriptBlockLogging
author: frack113
description: Detect malicious GPO modifications can be used to implement many other malicious behaviors.

name: SIGMA - Powershell MsXml COM Object
author: frack113, MatilJ
description:
Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
PowerShell is a powerful interactive command-line interface and scripting environment included in the Windows operating system. (Citation: TechNet PowerShell)
Adversaries can use PowerShell to perform a number of actions, including discovery of information and execution of code
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ComObject*" and ScriptBlockText:"*MsXml2.*" and ScriptBlockText:"*XmlHttp*"

name: SIGMA - Malicious Nishang PowerShell Commandlets
author: Alec Costello
description: Detects Commandlet names and arguments from the Nishang exploitation framework

name: SIGMA - NTFS Alternate Data Stream
author: Sami Ruohonen
description: Detects writing data into NTFS alternate data streams from powershell. Needs Script Block Logging.

name: SIGMA - Code Executed Via Office Add-in XLL File
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may abuse Microsoft Office add-ins to obtain persistence on a compromised system.
Office add-ins can be used to add functionality to Office programs
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*new-object *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ComObject *" and ScriptBlockText:"*.application*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.RegisterXLL*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Packet Capture Activity Via Start-NetEventSession - ScriptBlock
author: frack113
description:
Detects the execution of powershell scripts with calls to the "Start-NetEventSession" cmdlet. Which allows an attacker to start event and packet capture for a network event session.
Adversaries may attempt to capture network to gather information over the course of an operation.
Data captured via this technique may include user credentials, especially those sent over an insecure, unencrypted protocol.

name: SIGMA - Potential Invoke-Mimikatz PowerShell Script
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects Invoke-Mimikatz PowerShell script and alike. Mimikatz is a credential dumper capable of obtaining plaintext Windows account logins and passwords.
false_positives: Mimikatz can be useful for testing the security of networks
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*DumpCreds*" and ScriptBlockText:"*DumpCerts*") or ScriptBlockText:"*sekurlsa::logonpasswords*" or (ScriptBlockText:"*crypto::certificates*" and ScriptBlockText:"*CERT_SYSTEM_STORE_LOCAL_MACHINE*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Unconstrained Delegation Discovery Via Get-ADComputer - ScriptBlock
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of the "Get-ADComputer" cmdlet in order to identify systems which are configured for unconstrained delegation.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the library for administrative activity

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Web Access Installation - PsScript
author: Michael Haag
description: Detects the installation and configuration of PowerShell Web Access, which could be used for remote access and potential abuse
false_positives: Legitimate PowerShell Web Access installations by administrators
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Install-WindowsFeature WindowsPowerShellWebAccess*" or ScriptBlockText:"*Install-PswaWebApplication*" or (ScriptBlockText:"*Add-PswaAuthorizationRule*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-UserName *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ComputerName *")

name: SIGMA - PowerView PowerShell Cmdlets - ScriptBlock
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects Cmdlet names from PowerView of the PowerSploit exploitation framework.

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Credential Prompt
author: John Lambert (idea), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell calling a credential prompt

name: SIGMA - PSAsyncShell - Asynchronous TCP Reverse Shell
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of PSAsyncShell an Asynchronous TCP Reverse Shell written in powershell

name: SIGMA - PowerShell PSAttack
author: Sean Metcalf (source), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of PSAttack PowerShell hack tool

name: SIGMA - Potential RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.EXE Abuse - PowerShell ScriptBlock
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell module creation where the module Contents are set to "function Get-VMRemoteFXPhysicalVideoAdapter". This could be a sign of potential abuse of the "RemoteFXvGPUDisablement.exe" binary which is known to be vulnerable to module load-order hijacking.

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Remote Session Creation
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
PowerShell is a powerful interactive command-line interface and scripting environment included in the Windows operating system

name: SIGMA - Request A Single Ticket via PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
utilize native PowerShell Identity modules to query the domain to extract the Service Principal Names for a single computer.
This behavior is typically used during a kerberos or silver ticket attack.
A successful execution will output the SPNs for the endpoint in question.

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Script With File Hostname Resolving Capabilities
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell scripts that have capabilities to read files, loop through them and resolve DNS host entries.
false_positives: The same functionality can be implemented by admin scripts, correlate with name and creator
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Get-content *" and ScriptBlockText:"*foreach*" and ScriptBlockText:"*[System.Net.Dns]::GetHostEntry*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Out-File*"

name: SIGMA - Root Certificate Installed - PowerShell
author: oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st
description: Adversaries may install a root certificate on a compromised system to avoid warnings when connecting to adversary controlled web servers.
false_positives: Help Desk or IT may need to manually add a corporate Root CA on occasion. Need to test if GPO push doesn't trigger FP

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Invoke-Item From Mount-DiskImage
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may abuse container files such as disk image (.iso, .vhd) file formats to deliver malicious payloads that may not be tagged with MOTW.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Mount-DiskImage *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ImagePath *" and ScriptBlockText:"*Get-Volume*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.DriveLetter*" and ScriptBlockText:"*invoke-item *" and ScriptBlockText:"*):\\*"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Script With File Upload Capabilities
author: frack113
description: Detects PowerShell scripts leveraging the "Invoke-WebRequest" cmdlet to send data via either "PUT" or "POST" method.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Invoke-RestMethod*", "*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*irm *", "*iwr *")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*-Method \"POST\"*", "*-Method \"PUT\"*", "*-Method POST*", "*-Method PUT*", "*-Method 'POST'*", "*-Method 'PUT'*"))

name: SIGMA - Powershell Sensitive File Discovery
author: frack113
description: Detect adversaries enumerate sensitive files
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*ls*", "*get-childitem*", "*gci*")) and ScriptBlockText:"*-recurse*" and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*.pass*", "*.kdbx*", "*.kdb*"))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Script Change Permission Via Set-Acl - PsScript
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell scripts set ACL to of a file or a folder
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Set-Acl *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-AclObject *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Path *"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Set-Acl On Windows Folder - PsScript
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell scripts to set the ACL to a file in the Windows folder
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-Acl *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-AclObject *") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*-Path \"C:\\Windows*", "*-Path \"C:/Windows*", "*-Path 'C:\\Windows*", "*-Path 'C:/Windows*", "*-Path C:\\Windows*", "*-Path C:/Windows*", "*-Path $env:windir*", "*-Path \"$env:windir*", "*-Path '$env:windir*")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*FullControl*", "*Allow*"))

name: SIGMA - Change PowerShell Policies to an Insecure Level - PowerShell
author: frack113
description: Detects changing the PowerShell script execution policy to a potentially insecure level using the "Set-ExecutionPolicy" cmdlet.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-ExecutionPolicy*" and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Unrestricted*", "*bypass*"))) and (not (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://community.chocolatey.org/install.ps1')*", "*(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://chocolatey.org/install.ps1')*")))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell ShellCode
author: David Ledbetter (shellcode), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Base64 encoded Shellcode

name: SIGMA - Malicious ShellIntel PowerShell Commandlets
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Commandlet names from ShellIntel exploitation scripts.

name: SIGMA - Detected Windows Software Discovery - PowerShell
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Adversaries may attempt to enumerate software for a variety of reasons, such as figuring out what security measures are present or if the compromised system has a version of software that is vulnerable.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*get-itemProperty*" and ScriptBlockText:"*\\software\\*" and ScriptBlockText:"*select-object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*format-table*"

name: SIGMA - Powershell Store File In Alternate Data Stream
author: frack113
description: Storing files in Alternate Data Stream (ADS) similar to Astaroth malware.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Start-Process*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-FilePath \"$env:comspec\" *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ArgumentList *" and ScriptBlockText:"*>*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via Security Descriptors - ScriptBlock
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of certain functions and keywords that are used to manipulate security descriptors in order to potentially set a backdoor. As seen used in the DAMP project.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*win32_Trustee*" and ScriptBlockText:"*win32_Ace*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.AccessMask*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.AceType*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.SetSecurityDescriptor*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*\\Lsa\\JD*", "*\\Lsa\\Skew1*", "*\\Lsa\\Data*", "*\\Lsa\\GBG*"))

name: SIGMA - AD Groups Or Users Enumeration Using PowerShell - ScriptBlock
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may attempt to find domain-level groups and permission settings.
The knowledge of domain-level permission groups can help adversaries determine which groups exist and which users belong to a particular group.
Adversaries may use this information to determine which users have elevated permissions, such as domain administrators.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*get-ADPrincipalGroupMembership*" or (ScriptBlockText:"*get-aduser*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-f *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-pr *" and ScriptBlockText:"*DoesNotRequirePreAuth*")

name: SIGMA - Potential PowerShell Obfuscation Using Character Join
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects specific techniques often seen used inside of PowerShell scripts to obfscuate Alias creation

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Eventlog Clear
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of known powershell cmdlets such as "Clear-EventLog" to clear the Windows event logs
false_positives: Rare need to clear logs before doing something. Sometimes used by installers or cleaner scripts. The script should be investigated to determine if it's legitimate
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Clear-EventLog *", "*Remove-EventLog *", "*Limit-EventLog *", "*Clear-WinEvent *")) or (ScriptBlockText:"*Eventing.Reader.EventLogSession*" and ScriptBlockText:"*ClearLog*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*Diagnostics.EventLog*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Clear*")

name: SIGMA - Powershell Directory Enumeration
author: frack113
description: Detects technique used by MAZE ransomware to enumerate directories using Powershell
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*foreach*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ChildItem*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Path *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ErrorAction *" and ScriptBlockText:"*SilentlyContinue*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Out-File *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-append*"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Download - Powershell Script
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell download command
false_positives: PowerShell scripts that download content from the Internet

name: SIGMA - Powershell Execute Batch Script
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may abuse the Windows command shell for execution.
The Windows command shell ([cmd](https://attack.mitre.org/software/S0106)) is the primary command prompt on Windows systems.
The Windows command prompt can be used to control almost any aspect of a system, with various permission levels required for different subsets of commands.
Batch files (ex: .bat or .cmd) also provide the shell with a list of sequential commands to run, as well as normal scripting operations such as conditionals and loops.
Common uses of batch files include long or repetitive tasks, or the need to run the same set of commands on multiple system

name: SIGMA - Extracting Information with PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may search local file systems and remote file shares for files containing insecurely stored credentials.
These can be files created by users to store their own credentials, shared credential stores for a group of individuals,
configuration files containing passwords for a system or service, or source code/binary files containing embedded passwords.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*ls*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -R*" and ScriptBlockText:"*select-string *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Pattern *"

name: SIGMA - Troubleshooting Pack Cmdlet Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "TroubleshootingPack" cmdlets to leverage CVE-2022-30190 or action similar to "msdt" lolbin (as described in LOLBAS)
false_positives: Legitimate usage of "TroubleshootingPack" cmdlet for troubleshooting purposes
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Invoke-TroubleshootingPack*" and ScriptBlockText:"*C:\\Windows\\Diagnostics\\System\\PCW*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-AnswerFile*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Unattended*"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Get-Process LSASS in ScriptBlock
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a Get-Process command on lsass process, which is in almost all cases a sign of malicious activity
false_positives: Legitimate certificate exports invoked by administrators or users (depends on processes in the environment - filter if unusable)

name: SIGMA - Suspicious GetTypeFromCLSID ShellExecute
author: frack113
description: Detects suspicious Powershell code that execute COM Objects

name: SIGMA - Password Policy Discovery With Get-AdDefaultDomainPasswordPolicy
author: frack113
description: Detetcts PowerShell activity in which Get-Addefaultdomainpasswordpolicy is used to get the default password policy for an Active Directory domain.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Get Current User
author: frack113
description: Detects the use of PowerShell to identify the current logged user.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious GPO Discovery With Get-GPO
author: frack113
description: Detect use of Get-GPO to get one GPO or all the GPOs in a domain.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Process Discovery With Get-Process
author: frack113
description: Get the processes that are running on the local computer.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Hyper-V Cmdlets
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may carry out malicious operations using a virtual instance to avoid detection

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Invocations - Generic
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation command parameters
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* -enc *", "* -EncodedCommand *", "* -ec *")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* -w hidden *", "* -window hidden *", "* -windowstyle hidden *", "* -w 1 *")) and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* -noni *", "* -noninteractive *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell Invocations - Specific
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro
description: Detects suspicious PowerShell invocation command parameters
query: any where ((ScriptBlockText:"*-nop*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -w *" and ScriptBlockText:"*hidden*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -c *" and ScriptBlockText:"*[Convert]::FromBase64String*") or (ScriptBlockText:"* -w *" and ScriptBlockText:"*hidden*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-noni*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-nop*" and ScriptBlockText:"* -c *" and ScriptBlockText:"*iex*" and ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*") or (ScriptBlockText:"* -w *" and ScriptBlockText:"*hidden*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ep*" and ScriptBlockText:"*bypass*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Enc*") or ((ScriptBlockText:"*powershell*" and ScriptBlockText:"*reg*" and ScriptBlockText:"*add*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*\\software\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\run*", "*\\software\\wow6432node\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\run*", "*\\software\\microsoft\\windows\\currentversion\\policies\\explorer\\run*"))) or (ScriptBlockText:"*bypass*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-noprofile*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-windowstyle*" and ScriptBlockText:"*hidden*" and ScriptBlockText:"*new-object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*system.net.webclient*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.download*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*iex*" and ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Net.WebClient*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.Download*")) and (not (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://community.chocolatey.org/install.ps1*", "*(New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://chocolatey.org/install.ps1')*", "*Write-ChocolateyWarning*")))

name: SIGMA - Change User Agents with WebRequest
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols associated with web traffic to avoid detection/network filtering by blending in with existing traffic.
Commands to the remote system, and often the results of those commands, will be embedded within the protocol traffic between the client and server.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*Invoke-RestMethod*", "* irm *", "*iwr *")) and ScriptBlockText:"*-UserAgent *"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious IO.FileStream
author: frack113
description: Open a handle on the drive volume via the \\.\ DOS device path specifier and perform direct access read of the first few bytes of the volume.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*IO.FileStream*" and ScriptBlockText:"*\\\\.\\*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Keylogger Activity
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell scripts that contains reference to keystroke capturing functions

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious PowerShell Keywords
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Perez Diego (@darkquassar), Tuan Le (NCSGroup)
description: Detects potentially suspicious keywords that could indicate the use of a PowerShell exploitation framework

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Get Local Groups Information - PowerShell
author: frack113
description:
Detects the use of PowerShell modules and cmdlets to gather local group information.
Adversaries may use local system permission groups to determine which groups exist and which users belong to a particular group such as the local administrators group.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*get-localgroup *", "*get-localgroupmember *")) or ((ScriptBlockText like~ ("*get-wmiobject *", "*gwmi *", "*get-ciminstance *", "*gcim *")) and ScriptBlockText:"*win32_group*")

name: SIGMA - Powershell Local Email Collection
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may target user email on local systems to collect sensitive information.
Files containing email data can be acquired from a users local system, such as Outlook storage or cache files.

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Deleted Mounted Share
author: oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st
description: Detects when when a mounted share is removed. Adversaries may remove share connections that are no longer useful in order to clean up traces of their operation
false_positives: Administrators or Power users may remove their shares via cmd line
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Remove-SmbShare*", "*Remove-FileShare*")) and (not (ScriptBlockText:"*FileShare.cdxml*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Microsoft.PowerShell.Core\\Export-ModuleMember*" and ScriptBlockText:"*ROOT/Microsoft/Windows/Storage/MSFT_FileShare*" and ScriptBlockText:"*ObjectModelWrapper*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Cmdletization.MethodParameter*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Mount-DiskImage
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may abuse container files such as disk image (.iso, .vhd) file formats to deliver malicious payloads that may not be tagged with MOTW.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Connection to Remote Account
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries with no prior knowledge of legitimate credentials within the system or environment may guess passwords to attempt access to accounts.
Without knowledge of the password for an account, an adversary may opt to systematically guess the password using a repetitive or iterative mechanism

name: SIGMA - Suspicious New-PSDrive to Admin Share
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may use to interact with a remote network share using Server Message Block (SMB). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*New-PSDrive*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-psprovider *" and ScriptBlockText:"*filesystem*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-root *" and ScriptBlockText:"*\\\\*" and ScriptBlockText:"*$*"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious TCP Tunnel Via PowerShell Script
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects powershell scripts that creates sockets/listeners which could be indicative of tunneling activity

name: SIGMA - Recon Information for Export with PowerShell
author: frack113
description: Once established within a system or network, an adversary may use automated techniques for collecting internal data

name: SIGMA - Remove Account From Domain Admin Group
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may interrupt availability of system and network resources by inhibiting access to accounts utilized by legitimate users.
Accounts may be deleted, locked, or manipulated (ex: changed credentials) to remove access to accounts.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Remove-ADGroupMember*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Identity *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Members *"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Service DACL Modification Via Set-Service Cmdlet - PS
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "Set-Service" powershell cmdlet to configure a new SecurityDescriptor that allows a service to be hidden from other utilities such as "sc.exe", "Get-Service"...etc. (Works only in powershell 7)
false_positives: Rare intended use of hidden services
Rare FP could occur due to the non linearity of the ScriptBlockText log
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*-SecurityDescriptorSddl *", "*-sd *")) and ((ScriptBlockText:"*Set-Service *" and ScriptBlockText:"*D;;*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*;;;IU*", "*;;;SU*", "*;;;BA*", "*;;;SY*", "*;;;WD*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential PowerShell Obfuscation Using Alias Cmdlets
author: frack113
description: Detects Set-Alias or New-Alias cmdlet usage. Which can be use as a mean to obfuscate PowerShell scripts
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Set-Alias *", "*New-Alias *")) and (not (ScriptBlockText like~ ("Set-Alias -Name ncms -Value New-CimSession -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name gcls -Value Get-CimClass -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name ncso -Value New-CimSessionOption -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name gcms -Value Get-CimSession -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name rcms -Value Remove-cimSession -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name rcie -Value Register-CimIndicationEvent -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name gcai -Value Get-CimAssociatedInstance -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name gcim -Value Get-CimInstance -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name scim -Value Set-CimInstance -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name ncim -Value New-CimInstance -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name rcim -Value Remove-cimInstance -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue", "Set-Alias -Name icim -Value Invoke-CimMethod -Option ReadOnly, AllScope -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Get Information for SMB Share
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems as a means of identifying sources of information to gather as
a precursor for Collection and to identify potential systems of interest for Lateral Movement.
Networks often contain shared network drives and folders that enable users to access file directories on various systems across a network.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious SSL Connection
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may employ a known encryption algorithm to conceal command and control traffic rather than relying on any inherent protections provided by a communication protocol.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Start-Process PassThru
author: frack113
description: Powershell use PassThru option to start in background
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Start-Process*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-PassThru *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-FilePath *"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Unblock-File
author: frack113
description: Remove the Zone.Identifier alternate data stream which identifies the file as downloaded from the internet.

name: SIGMA - Replace Desktop Wallpaper by Powershell
author: frack113
description:
An adversary may deface systems internal to an organization in an attempt to intimidate or mislead users.
This may take the form of modifications to internal websites, or directly to user systems with the replacement of the desktop wallpaper
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ItemProperty*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Registry::*" and ScriptBlockText:"*HKEY_CURRENT_USER\\Control Panel\\Desktop\\*" and ScriptBlockText:"*WallPaper*") or ScriptBlockText:"*SystemParametersInfo(20,0,*,3)*"

name: SIGMA - Powershell Suspicious Win32_PnPEntity
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may attempt to gather information about attached peripheral devices and components connected to a computer system.

name: SIGMA - Deletion of Volume Shadow Copies via WMI with PowerShell - PS Script
author: Tim Rauch, frack113
description: Detects deletion of Windows Volume Shadow Copies with PowerShell code and Get-WMIObject. This technique is used by numerous ransomware families such as Sodinokibi/REvil
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Get-WmiObject*", "*gwmi*", "*Get-CimInstance*", "*gcim*")) and ScriptBlockText:"*Win32_ShadowCopy*" and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*.Delete()*", "*Remove-WmiObject*", "*rwmi*", "*Remove-CimInstance*", "*rcim*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PowerShell WindowStyle Option
author: frack113, Tim Shelton (fp AWS)
description:
Adversaries may use hidden windows to conceal malicious activity from the plain sight of users.
In some cases, windows that would typically be displayed when an application carries out an operation can be hidden
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*powershell*" and ScriptBlockText:"*WindowStyle*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Hidden*") and (not (ScriptBlockText:"*:\\Program Files\\Amazon\\WorkSpacesConfig\\Scripts\\*" and ScriptBlockText:"*$PSScriptRoot\\Module\\WorkspaceScriptModule\\WorkspaceScriptModule*"))

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Write-EventLog Usage
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "Write-EventLog" cmdlet with 'RawData' flag. The cmdlet can be levreage to write malicious payloads to the EventLog and then retrieve them later for later use
false_positives: Legitimate applications writing events via this cmdlet. Investigate alerts to determine if the action is benign

name: SIGMA - Zip A Folder With PowerShell For Staging In Temp - PowerShell Script
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
description:
Detects PowerShell scripts that make use of the "Compress-Archive" Cmdlet in order to compress folders and files where the output is stored in a potentially suspicious location that is used often by malware for exfiltration.
An adversary might compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.

name: SIGMA - SyncAppvPublishingServer Execution to Bypass Powershell Restriction
author: Ensar Şamil, @sblmsrsn, OSCD Community
description: Detects SyncAppvPublishingServer process execution which usually utilized by adversaries to bypass PowerShell execution restrictions.

name: SIGMA - Tamper Windows Defender Remove-MpPreference - ScriptBlockLogging
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects attempts to remove Windows Defender configuration using the 'MpPreference' cmdlet

name: SIGMA - Tamper Windows Defender - ScriptBlockLogging
author: frack113, elhoim, Tim Shelton (fps, alias support), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PowerShell scripts attempting to disable scheduled scanning and other parts of Windows Defender ATP or set default actions to allow.
false_positives: Legitimate PowerShell scripts that disable Windows Defender for troubleshooting purposes. Must be investigated.
