query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-MpPreference*" and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*-dbaf $true*", "*-dbaf 1*", "*-dbm $true*", "*-dbm 1*", "*-dips $true*", "*-dips 1*", "*-DisableArchiveScanning $true*", "*-DisableArchiveScanning 1*", "*-DisableBehaviorMonitoring $true*", "*-DisableBehaviorMonitoring 1*", "*-DisableBlockAtFirstSeen $true*", "*-DisableBlockAtFirstSeen 1*", "*-DisableCatchupFullScan $true*", "*-DisableCatchupFullScan 1*", "*-DisableCatchupQuickScan $true*", "*-DisableCatchupQuickScan 1*", "*-DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem $true*", "*-DisableIntrusionPreventionSystem 1*", "*-DisableIOAVProtection $true*", "*-DisableIOAVProtection 1*", "*-DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true*", "*-DisableRealtimeMonitoring 1*", "*-DisableRemovableDriveScanning $true*", "*-DisableRemovableDriveScanning 1*", "*-DisableScanningMappedNetworkDrivesForFullScan $true*", "*-DisableScanningMappedNetworkDrivesForFullScan 1*", "*-DisableScanningNetworkFiles $true*", "*-DisableScanningNetworkFiles 1*", "*-DisableScriptScanning $true*", "*-DisableScriptScanning 1*", "*-MAPSReporting $false*", "*-MAPSReporting 0*", "*-drdsc $true*", "*-drdsc 1*", "*-drtm $true*", "*-drtm 1*", "*-dscrptsc $true*", "*-dscrptsc 1*", "*-dsmndf $true*", "*-dsmndf 1*", "*-dsnf $true*", "*-dsnf 1*", "*-dss $true*", "*-dss 1*"))) or (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-MpPreference*" and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*HighThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*htdefac Allow*", "*LowThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*ltdefac Allow*", "*ModerateThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*mtdefac Allow*", "*SevereThreatDefaultAction Allow*", "*stdefac Allow*")))

name: SIGMA - Testing Usage of Uncommonly Used Port
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may communicate using a protocol and port paring that are typically not associated.
For example, HTTPS over port 8088(Citation: Symantec Elfin Mar 2019) or port 587(Citation: Fortinet Agent Tesla April 2018) as opposed to the traditional port 443.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Test-NetConnection*" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ComputerName *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-port *") and (not (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* 443 *", "* 80 *")))

name: SIGMA - Powershell Timestomp
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may modify file time attributes to hide new or changes to existing files.
Timestomping is a technique that modifies the timestamps of a file (the modify, access, create, and change times), often to mimic files that are in the same folder.

name: SIGMA - Powershell Token Obfuscation - Powershell
author: frack113
description: Detects TOKEN OBFUSCATION technique from Invoke-Obfuscation
query: any where (ScriptBlockText regex~ "\w+`(\w+|-|.)`[\w+|\s]" or ScriptBlockText regex~ ""(\{\d\}){2,}"\s*-f" or ScriptBlockText regex~ "(?i)\$\{`?e`?n`?v`?:`?p`?a`?t`?h`?\}") and (not (ScriptBlockText:"*${env:path}*" or (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*it will return true or false instead*", "*The function also prevents `Get-ItemProperty` from failing*")) or (Path:"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Exchange Server\\*" and Path:"*\\bin\\servicecontrol.ps1" and ScriptBlockText:"*`r`n*")))

name: SIGMA - User Discovery And Export Via Get-ADUser Cmdlet - PowerShell
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the Get-ADUser cmdlet to collect user information and output it to a file
false_positives: Legitimate admin scripts may use the same technique, it's better to exclude specific computers or users who execute these commands or scripts often
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Get-ADUser *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Filter \**") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* > *", "* | Select *", "*Out-File*", "*Set-Content*", "*Add-Content*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Persistence Via PowerShell User Profile Using Add-Content
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects calls to "Add-Content" cmdlet in order to modify the content of the user profile and potentially adding suspicious commands for persistence
false_positives: Legitimate administration and tuning scripts that aim to add functionality to a user PowerShell session

name: SIGMA - Abuse of Service Permissions to Hide Services Via Set-Service - PS
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the "Set-Service" powershell cmdlet to configure a new SecurityDescriptor that allows a service to be hidden from other utilities such as "sc.exe", "Get-Service"...etc. (Works only in powershell 7)
false_positives: Rare intended use of hidden services
Rare FP could occur due to the non linearity of the ScriptBlockText log
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-Service *" and ScriptBlockText:"*DCLCWPDTSD*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*-SecurityDescriptorSddl *", "*-sd *"))

name: SIGMA - Veeam Backup Servers Credential Dumping Script Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of a PowerShell script that contains calls to the "Veeam.Backup" class, in order to dump stored credentials.
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*[Credentials]*" and ScriptBlockText:"*[Veeam.Backup.Common.ProtectedStorage]::GetLocalString*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Invoke-Sqlcmd*" and ScriptBlockText:"*Veeam Backup and Replication*"

name: SIGMA - Usage Of Web Request Commands And Cmdlets - ScriptBlock
author: James Pemberton / @4A616D6573
description: Detects the use of various web request commands with commandline tools and Windows PowerShell cmdlets (including aliases) via PowerShell scriptblock logs
false_positives: Use of Get-Command and Get-Help modules to reference Invoke-WebRequest and Start-BitsTransfer.

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Call To Win32_NTEventlogFile Class - PSScript
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the WMI class "Win32_NTEventlogFile" in a potentially suspicious way (delete, backup, change permissions, etc.) from a PowerShell script

name: SIGMA - PowerShell WMI Win32_Product Install MSI
author: frack113
description: Detects the execution of an MSI file using PowerShell and the WMI Win32_Product class
query: any where ScriptBlockText:"*Invoke-CimMethod *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ClassName *" and ScriptBlockText:"*Win32_Product *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-MethodName *" and ScriptBlockText:"*.msi*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Firewall Profile Disabled
author: Austin Songer @austinsonger
description: Detects when a user disables the Windows Firewall via a Profile to help evade defense.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*Set-NetFirewallProfile *" and ScriptBlockText:"* -Enabled *" and ScriptBlockText:"* False*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("* -All *", "*Public*", "*Domain*", "*Private*"))

name: SIGMA - Winlogon Helper DLL
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description:
Winlogon.exe is a Windows component responsible for actions at logon/logoff as well as the secure attention sequence (SAS) triggered by Ctrl-Alt-Delete.
Registry entries in HKLM\Software[Wow6432Node]Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\ and HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\ are
used to manage additional helper programs and functionalities that support Winlogon. Malicious modifications to these Registry keys may cause Winlogon to
load and execute malicious DLLs and/or executables.

name: SIGMA - Potential WinAPI Calls Via PowerShell Scripts
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects use of WinAPI functions in PowerShell scripts
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*VirtualAlloc*" and ScriptBlockText:"*OpenProcess*" and ScriptBlockText:"*WriteProcessMemory*" and ScriptBlockText:"*CreateRemoteThread*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*OpenProcessToken*" and ScriptBlockText:"*LookupPrivilegeValue*" and ScriptBlockText:"*AdjustTokenPrivileges*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*OpenProcessToken*" and ScriptBlockText:"*DuplicateTokenEx*" and ScriptBlockText:"*CloseHandle*") or (ScriptBlockText:"*WriteProcessMemory*" and ScriptBlockText:"*VirtualAlloc*" and ScriptBlockText:"*ReadProcessMemory*" and ScriptBlockText:"*VirtualFree*")

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Exclusions Added - PowerShell
author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
description: Detects modifications to the Windows Defender configuration settings using PowerShell to add exclusions

name: SIGMA - WMImplant Hack Tool
author: NVISO
description: Detects parameters used by WMImplant
false_positives: Administrative scripts that use the same keywords.
query: any where ScriptBlockText like~ ("*WMImplant*", "* change_user *", "* gen_cli *", "* command_exec *", "* disable_wdigest *", "* disable_winrm *", "* enable_wdigest *", "* enable_winrm *", "* registry_mod *", "* remote_posh *", "* sched_job *", "* service_mod *", "* process_kill *", "* active_users *", "* basic_info *", "* power_off *", "* vacant_system *", "* logon_events *")

name: SIGMA - Powershell WMI Persistence
author: frack113
description: Adversaries may establish persistence and elevate privileges by executing malicious content triggered by a Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) event subscription.
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*New-CimInstance *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Namespace root/subscription *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ClassName __EventFilter *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Property *") or (ScriptBlockText:"*New-CimInstance *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Namespace root/subscription *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-ClassName CommandLineEventConsumer *" and ScriptBlockText:"*-Property *")

name: SIGMA - WMIC Unquoted Services Path Lookup - PowerShell
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects known WMI recon method to look for unquoted service paths, often used by pentest inside of powershell scripts attackers enum scripts
query: any where (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*Get-WmiObject *", "*gwmi *")) and (ScriptBlockText:"* Win32_Service *" and ScriptBlockText:"*Name*" and ScriptBlockText:"*DisplayName*" and ScriptBlockText:"*PathName*" and ScriptBlockText:"*StartMode*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious X509Enrollment - Ps Script
author: frack113
description: Detect use of X509Enrollment

name: SIGMA - Powershell XML Execute Command
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
PowerShell is a powerful interactive command-line interface and scripting environment included in the Windows operating system. (Citation: TechNet PowerShell)
Adversaries can use PowerShell to perform a number of actions, including discovery of information and execution of code
query: any where (ScriptBlockText:"*New-Object*" and ScriptBlockText:"*System.Xml.XmlDocument*" and ScriptBlockText:"*.Load*") and (ScriptBlockText like~ ("*IEX *", "*Invoke-Expression *", "*Invoke-Command *", "*ICM -*"))
